moments.name: Ladle Inspection
moments.description: Introduction to ladle inspection procedures before tapping operations begin.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Welcome to EAF steel tapping training. Before we begin the tapping process, we must first inspect the ladle to ensure it's ready for molten steel. The ladle must be preheated to the correct temperature and structurally sound. Let's examine the key inspection points.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Check the ladle refractory lining for any cracks or damage. Verify the preheating temperature meets specifications. Inspect the ladle bottom and walls for structural integrity. Confirm all safety mechanisms are operational.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. You now understand the critical inspection points for ladle preparation. Next, we'll position the ladle under the furnace for tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel plant ladle preheating station with large refractory-lined ladle, worker inspecting the vessel under ambient furnace lighting

moments.name: Ladle Top Ring Check
moments.description: Narrator explains the importance of inspecting the ladle top ring for damage before tapping.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Before we begin the tapping process, we need to inspect the ladle top ring. Check carefully for any cracks, warping, or heat damage. The top ring must be in good condition to ensure a safe seal during metal transfer. Any structural weakness here could lead to dangerous leaks or spills during tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. The ladle top ring inspection is complete. We can now proceed to the next pre-tap preparation step.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial ladle preheating station with large steel ladle visible, top ring highlighted for inspection, ambient furnace glow in background

moments.name: Ferroalloy Bunker Readiness
moments.description: Inspect ferroalloy hopper to confirm materials are loaded before tapping begins.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Before we begin tapping, you need to verify that the ferroalloy bunker is loaded with the required materials. Walk up to the ferroalloy hopper and inspect it to confirm readiness.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. The ferroalloy bunker is loaded and ready. Materials are available for addition during the tapping process.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You need to inspect the ferroalloy hopper. It's the highlighted structure ahead of you. Touch it to perform the inspection.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The ferroalloy hopper is directly in front of you. Reach out and touch it with your hand to complete the inspection.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Pre-tap preparation is complete. All systems are verified and ready. Let's proceed to the next phase.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF tapping area with ferroalloy hopper positioned at waist height, player reaching forward to inspect loaded materials under bright factory lighting

moments.name: Local Control Switch ON
moments.description: Activate local controls on the EAF tapping control panel

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You are now at the EAF tapping control panel. Before we begin the tapping sequence, you must activate the local control switch. This transfers control authority from the central system to this panel, allowing you to manually operate the furnace tapping process. Look for the local control switch on the main console at chest height.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Reach out and flip the local control switch to the ON position. You'll see an indicator light illuminate when control is successfully activated.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent! The local control switch is now ON. You can see the indicator light is illuminated, confirming that you have control authority at this panel. You're now ready to proceed with the tapping sequence.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to flip the local control switch to activate it. Grab the switch with your controller and squeeze the trigger to flip it to the ON position.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The local control switch is directly in front of you on the main console. Reach out with either hand, grab the switch, and squeeze your controller trigger to flip it ON. You'll see an indicator light when it activates.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect work! The local controls are now active. In the next step, you'll prepare the ladle car for positioning under the EAF tap hole.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF control panel with illuminated local control switch, furnace glowing in background, operator perspective at chest-height console

moments.name: Ladle Car Positioning
moments.description: Position the ladle transfer car under the EAF tapping spout

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we need to position the ladle transfer car directly under the furnace tapping spout. Move the car along the track until it locks into the correct position.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good, you've grabbed the car. Now slide it along the track toward the tapping position under the furnace.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect! The ladle car is now locked in position directly under the EAF tapping spout. You heard that solid clunk—that's the locking mechanism engaging.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"That's not the correct position. The ladle car must be centered directly under the furnace tapping spout. Try again.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, the ladle car needs to be positioned precisely under the tapping spout. Look for the track alignment markers.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Slide the ladle car along the track until it's directly beneath the furnace EBT opening. You'll feel resistance when it reaches the locking position.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. The ladle car is secure and ready for tapping. Next, we'll prepare the tapping controls.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF tapping bay with ladle transfer car on floor-level tracks positioned beneath the elevated furnace tapping spout, ambient orange glow from furnace, metal framework and safety railings visible

moments.name: Material Addition Chute Positioning
moments.description: Narration explains the role and positioning of the material addition chute in the EAF tapping process.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The material addition chute is now being positioned above the ladle. This chute allows precise delivery of ferroalloys and other additives during the tapping process. Proper positioning ensures accurate material addition without spillage or contamination.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The chute must align vertically with the ladle opening and maintain clearance from the tapping stream. Operators verify positioning before each heat to prevent material loss and ensure metallurgical accuracy.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Material addition chute positioning complete. Next, we'll review the ferroalloys bunker system.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: EAF tapping side view with material addition chute highlighted above ladle opening, showing proper alignment and clearance for ferroalloy delivery during steel tapping operation.

moments.name: Bath Temperature Verification
moments.description: Operator learns the importance of verifying molten steel temperature before tapping.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Before we begin tapping, we must verify the bath temperature. The molten steel must reach the target temperature specified by the heat plan to ensure proper chemistry and flow characteristics. Temperature verification is a critical safety and quality checkpoint that prevents issues downstream in the steelmaking process.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Temperature verification complete. We're now ready to proceed with the tapping sequence.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF furnace area with glowing molten steel visible through observation ports, operator standing near temperature monitoring displays with ambient heat shimmer effects in the steel plant environment.

moments.name: Enable Tilting Control
moments.description: Insert and turn the furnace tilting key on the sampling console to enable tilt control.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now enable the furnace tilting control. Grab the tilting key and insert it into the keyhole on the sampling console, then turn it clockwise to enable tilting.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. Now insert the key into the keyhole on the sampling console.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect. The key is inserted. Now turn it clockwise to enable tilting control.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You need to keep holding the key to insert it into the console.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"That's not the correct location. Insert the key into the keyhole on the sampling console.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to grab the tilting key first, then insert it into the sampling console keyhole.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Let me show you. Grab the furnace tilting key with your right hand, place it into the keyhole on the sampling console, and turn it clockwise until you hear a click.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work! The furnace tilting control is now enabled. The console display confirms tilting is ready.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF tapping control area with operator standing at sampling console panel, furnace tilting key being inserted and turned in keyhole, console display showing TILTING ENABLED status

moments.name: Start Ladle Stirring
moments.description: Activate the stirring gas system by flipping either the Argon or Nitrogen selection switch on the pipe control panel.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we need to start the ladle stirring system. Locate the pipe control switches on the control panel to your right. You can activate either the Argon or Nitrogen gas selection switch to begin stirring.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. Argon gas is now flowing into the ladle. The stirring system is active and will help homogenize the molten steel composition.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. Nitrogen gas is now flowing into the ladle. The stirring system is active and will help homogenize the molten steel composition.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look for the gas selection switches on the pipe control panel. You need to activate either the Argon or Nitrogen switch to start stirring.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The gas selection switches are directly in front of you on the control panel. Grab either the Argon or Nitrogen switch and flip it to activate stirring.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Stirring system activated successfully. The gas flow will ensure proper mixing of the steel as we continue the tapping process.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF tapping control panel with illuminated switches at waist height, operator activating gas selection controls while molten steel flows into ladle below

moments.name: Unlock EBT Flap
moments.description: Press ExtrastrokeBYpass and Unlock_01 simultaneously to unlock the EBT flap mechanism.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"To unlock the EBT flap, you must press both the Extrastroke Bypass and Unlock buttons simultaneously on the pipe control switches. This releases the hydraulic lock on the eccentric bottom tapping system. Locate the buttons on the center console at chest height.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Extrastroke Bypass button pressed. Now press the Unlock button simultaneously.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. EBT flap unlocked. The hydraulic lock has been released and the system is ready for the opening sequence.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You released the Extrastroke Bypass button too early. Both buttons must be pressed simultaneously to unlock the EBT flap. Try again.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to press both the Extrastroke Bypass and Unlock buttons at the same time. Locate them on the center console and press them together.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"This is critical: press the Extrastroke Bypass button on the left and the Unlock button on the right simultaneously. Use both hands if needed. This is a safety interlock to prevent accidental EBT flap opening.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"EBT flap successfully unlocked. You are now ready to proceed with the opening sequence. Next, you will initiate the flap opening procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF control panel with chest-height buttons ExtrastrokeBYpass and Unlock_01 highlighted; player reaching toward dual button controls; steel furnace operations visible in background

moments.name: Open EBT Flap
moments.description: Press ExtrastrokeBYpass and Opening simultaneously to open the EBT flap and initiate steel flow from the furnace.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we need to open the EBT flap to allow steel to flow. Press the ExtrastrokeBYpass button and the Opening button simultaneously on the pipe control switches. Watch for sand to drop and molten steel to begin flowing.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent! The EBT flap is now open. You can see the sand dropping and molten steel beginning to flow into the ladle. The orange glow is intensifying as steel flows through the tapping hole.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to press both the ExtrastrokeBYpass button and the Opening button simultaneously to open the EBT flap.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look for the green highlighted buttons on the pipe control panel. You need to press ExtrastrokeBYpass and Opening together.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Let me guide you. The ExtrastrokeBYpass button is on the left, and the Opening button is on the right. Press both buttons at the same time to open the EBT flap.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect execution! The EBT flap is open and steel is now flowing into the ladle. You can see the characteristic orange glow and hear the flow of molten steel. Let's move to the next step.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF tapping control panel at chest height with ExtrastrokeBYpass and Opening buttons highlighted in green, furnace glowing orange in background as molten steel begins flowing into ladle with visible sand dropping and intense heat radiation

moments.name: Observe Steel Flow
moments.description: Visual confirmation of sand drop and molten steel flow from EBT into ladle

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Watch the EBT area carefully. You should see sand drop first, followed by the molten steel stream flowing into the ladle. Maintain a safe observation distance of 3 meters.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Steel flow initiated successfully. The stream appears consistent and the flow rate is within normal parameters. Continue monitoring the ladle fill level.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"EBT opening sequence complete. Steel is now flowing into the ladle as planned. We'll proceed to the next phase of the tapping process.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel furnace interior with glowing EBT tap hole, bright orange molten steel stream pouring into large ladle vessel below, safety observation deck 3 meters away with industrial lighting

moments.name: Oxygen Lance Preparation
moments.description: Grab and prepare the oxygen lancing pipe for emergency EBT opening procedure

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Emergency EBT opening procedure initiated. Locate the oxygen lancing pipe at the emergency equipment station. You'll need to grab and prepare it for use. The lance is stored vertically about 2 meters from the EBT area.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. Lance securely held. You're now ready for emergency use. Maintain a firm grip and proceed with caution.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Maintain your grip on the lancing pipe. In an emergency situation, you need to keep the lance secure at all times.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look for the oxygen lancing pipe. It's stored vertically at the emergency equipment station, highlighted in orange. Grab it with either hand.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"This is critical. Reach out and grab the oxygen lancing pipe now. It's directly in front of you at the emergency station. Use your grip button to secure it.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Lance ready. Proceed to the EBT area for emergency opening procedure.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Emergency equipment station near EBT area with vertical oxygen lancing pipe highlighted in orange, emergency alarm ambient lighting, worker reaching to grab the metal lance for emergency procedure

moments.name: Lance EBT Hole
moments.description: Use the lancing pipe to pierce and open the furnace EBT hole for emergency tapping

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Position the lancing pipe at a 45-degree angle approximately one meter from the EBT tap hole. Apply steady pressure to pierce through and initiate steel flow.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. Now position the lance at the EBT hole insertion point.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent positioning. Apply steady pressure to pierce through the refractory.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Emergency tapping successful! Steel flow has initiated. Monitor the stream and maintain safe distance from the molten metal.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"Incorrect approach. Remember to position the lance at a 45-degree angle to the EBT hole and apply steady, controlled pressure.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"First, grab the lancing pipe with both hands. Look for the highlighted EBT hole on the furnace.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Let me guide you. Grab the lancing pipe, position it at the marked insertion point on the furnace, then push it through the EBT hole opening.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Emergency EBT opening complete. The steel is now flowing into the ladle. This emergency procedure has been successfully executed.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Emergency tapping scene showing the lancing pipe positioned at a 45-degree angle piercing through the EBT hole of the electric arc furnace with molten steel beginning to flow out in a bright orange stream

moments.name: Add Ferroalloys
moments.description: Add ferroalloys through hopper when 20-25 tons of steel have been tapped into the ladle

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The tapping process is underway. Your next task is to add ferroalloys when the ladle contains 20 to 25 tons of tapped steel. Position yourself at the hopper control station and prepare to release the ferroalloys.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. Ferroalloys added successfully. The materials are now mixing with the tapped steel in the ladle. This addition will help achieve the target chemistry.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to operate the hopper control to release the ferroalloys into the ladle. Locate the release lever at the hopper station.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You need to add ferroalloys now. Approach the hopper control station, which is about 2 meters from the ladle position. The release lever is at chest height.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"This is critical. Grab and activate the ferroalloy hopper control now. The lever must be operated to release the materials into the ladle during tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Ferroalloy addition complete. The materials are now integrating with the molten steel. Next, we'll focus on additional process controls during tapping.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel mill tapping floor with ferroalloy hopper station positioned near large steel ladle, materials cascading from hopper into glowing molten steel with visible splash effects

moments.name: Park Material Chute
moments.description: Narration explaining the parking procedure for the material addition chute after tapping completion.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"After completing material additions during tapping, it's important to safely park the material addition chute. This prevents interference with other operations and keeps the work area clear for the next phase of the process.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The material chute is now parked and secured. Let's move on to the next step in the tapping process.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: EAF tapping area with the material addition chute being positioned in its parked location, clearing the workspace for continued operations.

moments.name: Tilt Furnace to Complete Tapping
moments.description: Trainee uses remote control joystick to tilt the furnace forward and complete the steel tapping process.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now use the remote control joystick to gradually tilt the furnace forward. This will complete the tapping process and drain the remaining steel into the ladle. Keep the tilt smooth and controlled.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good, you've activated the joystick. The furnace is beginning to tilt. Continue holding to complete the tapping process.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. The furnace is tilting and the remaining steel is draining into the ladle. The tapping process is nearly complete.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to use the joystick on the remote control to tilt the furnace. Grab the joystick and move it forward.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You must tilt the furnace to complete the tapping. Use the joystick on the remote control. Grip it with your hand and push it forward to tilt the furnace.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect. The furnace has been tilted and the tapping is complete. The steel has been successfully transferred to the ladle.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF furnace control station with remote joystick console, operator tilting large electric arc furnace forward as molten steel pours into waiting ladle below

moments.name: Tilt Back to Deslagging Side
moments.description: Use remote joystick to tilt furnace back to deslagging side when 5-7 tons remain in the ladle.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"With approximately 5 to 7 tons remaining in the ladle, it's time to tilt the furnace back to the deslagging side. Use the joystick on the remote control to perform this operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. Now manipulate the joystick to tilt the furnace back toward the deslagging position.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. The furnace has been successfully tilted back to the deslagging side.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Focus on the remote control joystick. Use it to tilt the furnace back to the deslagging position.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to grab and manipulate the joystick on the gunning and fettling remote to tilt the furnace back.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look at the remote control station highlighted in front of you. Grab the joystick and squeeze the trigger to activate the furnace tilt mechanism.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect execution. You have successfully tapped 150 to 155 tons of steel and returned the furnace to the deslagging position. This completes the tapping operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF facility with large electric arc furnace tilted at deslagging angle, operator using remote control joystick at control station 4 meters away, steam and heat radiating from furnace opening

moments.name: Move Ladle Clear
moments.description: Move the ladle transfer car away from the furnace to clear the tapping zone

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now that tapping is complete, you need to move the ladle transfer car away from the furnace. Interact with the ladle car controls to move it clear of the tapping zone.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. Now move the ladle car away from the furnace to clear the tapping zone.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. The ladle has been safely removed from the tapping zone. The furnace is now ready for the next operational phase.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to interact with the ladle car and move it away from the furnace to clear the tapping zone.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"To move the ladle car, grab it and pull it away from the furnace area. The car needs to be clear of the tapping zone.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look at the highlighted ladle transfer car. Grab it with your controller and move it away from the furnace until it clears the tapping zone.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Ladle safely removed. Tapping operation complete. The furnace is now ready for post-tapping procedures.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF steel plant tapping area with ladle transfer car positioned on rails moving away from large electric arc furnace after tapping completion

moments.name: Clean EBT Bottom
moments.description: Cycle the EBT flap to clean residual material using control panel buttons.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Before filling the EBT with sand, we need to clean any residual material from the bottom. Approach the control panel and use the Opening button to cycle the EBT flap to a 7-degree angle, then lock it in place with Lock_01.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. The EBT flap is cycling. Now press Lock_01 to secure the position.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"Remember, first press Opening to cycle the EBT flap, then use Lock_01 to secure it.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The control panel is directly in front of you. Look for the highlighted Opening button first.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Press the Opening button on the control panel to cycle the EBT flap. It's the green highlighted button at chest height.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. The EBT bottom is now cleaned and secured at the correct angle. The flap cycle has cleared any residual material, and we're ready for the next step.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF tapping control panel with operator pressing buttons to cycle the EBT flap mechanism, control interface highlighted with green and orange indicators at chest height in steel mill environment

moments.name: Close and Lock EBT Flap
moments.description: Close the EBT flap fully and press the lock button to secure the tap hole.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now close the EBT flap fully by pushing it into position, then press the lock button on the pipe control panel to secure it.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. The flap is now closed. Press the lock button to secure it.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"EBT locked closed. The tap hole is now securely sealed.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"Remember, first close the EBT flap completely, then press the lock button.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Push the EBT flap fully into its closed position, then press the lock button on the control panel.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look at the highlighted flap. Close it first by pushing it into place. Then press the green-highlighted lock button.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. The EBT flap is now closed and locked. We can proceed to the next step.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF tapping control panel with chest-high buttons; worker closes metal flap and presses green lock indicator

moments.name: Open EBT Cover and Inspect
moments.description: Open the Delta Roof Cover and verify tap hole condition using the camera display

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Before we begin sand filling, we must open the EBT cover and inspect the tap hole condition. First, approach the Delta Roof Cover controls and open the cover. Then verify the tap hole using the camera display.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. The Delta Roof Cover is now open. Now approach the furnace temperature display and verify the tap hole condition using the camera feed.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. Camera feed shows the tap hole is clear and ready for sand filling. Tap hole verified. You may now proceed to the next step.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"That's not the correct procedure. Remember, first open the Delta Roof Cover, then use the camera display to inspect the tap hole.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"You need to interact with the Delta Roof Cover controls. Look for the highlighted cover mechanism and use it to open the cover.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Use your controller to grab and activate the Delta Roof Cover control. Press the trigger button while pointing at the highlighted mechanism.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Well done. You have successfully opened the EBT cover and verified the tap hole condition. The area is now ready for sand filling operations.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: EBT platform area 2 meters above furnace floor with open Delta Roof Cover revealing tap hole; operator holding active camera display showing clear tap hole feed

moments.name: Fill EBT with Sand
moments.description: Use forklift sand gun to fill the EBT tap hole with a dome-shaped sand pile.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now you need to fill the EBT tap hole with sand. Position the forklift sand gun nozzle at the hole level, approximately 1.5 meters from the tap hole, and activate the sand gun to fill the hole with a dome-shaped pile.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good, you've picked up the sand gun. Now position it near the EBT tap hole and activate it to fill with sand.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Perfect! The sand is flowing into the EBT hole and forming the required dome shape. EBT filled correctly.","waitForCompletion":true}

onWrong.Name: VoiceOver
onWrong.Data: {"text":"That's not quite right. Make sure you position the sand gun nozzle at the tap hole level and maintain proper distance before activating.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, you need to grab the forklift sand gun and position it near the EBT tap hole at the correct level and distance.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Let me guide you. Pick up the sand gun with either hand, move it to approximately 1.5 meters from the tap hole, position the nozzle at hole level, and hold it there for 2 seconds to fill the EBT with sand.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work! You've successfully filled the EBT tap hole with sand, creating the dome-shaped pile required for proper closure. This prevents molten steel from flowing back during operations.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF area with forklift-mounted sand gun positioned near the EBT tap hole on the furnace base; operator actively filling the tap hole with flowing sand forming a protective dome-shaped pile.

moments.name: Close Sand Hopper and EBT Cover
moments.description: Player closes the sand hopper on the forklift and secures the EBT delta roof cover to seal the system for the next heat.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now close the sand hopper on the forklift, then secure the EBT delta roof cover. This seals the system for the next heat cycle.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good, hopper closed. Now secure the EBT delta roof cover.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember, close the sand hopper first, then secure the delta roof cover. Work in sequence.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Close the hopper on the forklift by activating its control, then secure the EBT cover by latching it into position.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent work. EBT sealed for next heat. The system is now secure and ready for the next tapping cycle.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EBT area with forklift sand hopper at seat level and delta roof cover at elevated platform height, operator securing closure mechanisms with mechanical latching sounds

moments.name: Estimate Hot Heel
moments.description: Operator learns about estimating the hot heel remaining in the furnace after tapping.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"After tapping is complete, it's important to estimate the hot heel remaining in the furnace. The hot heel is the residual molten steel left at the bottom, which helps maintain temperature for the next heat. Visual inspection and experience guide this estimation, typically ranging from 15 to 25 tons depending on furnace size and operational practice.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Proper hot heel management reduces energy consumption and improves heat-to-heat consistency. Too little hot heel requires more energy to reach tapping temperature; too much reduces the charge capacity for the next heat.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good. You now understand the importance of estimating the hot heel for efficient furnace operation. Let's proceed to the next housekeeping task.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Wide view of the EAF furnace interior after tapping, showing the glowing residual molten steel pool (hot heel) at the bottom with radiant heat waves rising in the dim industrial environment.

moments.name: Return Furnace to Horizontal
moments.description: Use the remote control joystick to return the furnace to its horizontal position after tapping.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Now we need to return the furnace to its horizontal position. Use the remote control joystick to level the furnace.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. Furnace is now horizontal and ready for the next heat cycle.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Remember to use the remote control joystick to level the furnace.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Look for the gunning and fettling remote control near the furnace. Use its joystick to return the furnace to horizontal.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Grab the joystick on the remote control and operate it to level the furnace back to its horizontal position.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Good work. The furnace is now horizontal and secured for the next operation.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial steel mill environment with a large electric arc furnace tilted at an angle, operator standing at a remote control station with joystick controls 4 meters away, preparing to level the furnace back to horizontal position

moments.name: Clean Slag Door
moments.description: Narration explaining the importance of slag door cleaning after tapping operations.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"After completing the tapping operation, it's critical to clean the slag door thoroughly. This prevents slag buildup that could interfere with future taps and ensures safe furnace operation. The slag door area must be inspected and any residual material removed before the next heat cycle begins.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Excellent. Maintaining clean slag door conditions is essential for operational safety and efficiency. Let's move to the final housekeeping task.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: EAF slag door area with residual slag visible, industrial furnace environment with safety barriers and maintenance equipment nearby

moments.name: Insert Shell Verticality Locks (Conditional)
moments.description: Player inserts ShellChange locks into Furnace shell to secure it for charging cycle.

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Final step before the next heat cycle. Insert the shell verticality locks into the furnace shell to secure it during charging. Each lock must engage fully until you hear the mechanical click.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Shell secured for charging. The furnace is now ready for the next heat cycle. Excellent work completing all post-tap housekeeping procedures.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"That lock position won't secure the shell properly. Look for the designated lock insertion points around the furnace base.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"The shell locks need to be inserted into the correct mounting points on the furnace shell. Look for the lock receptacles near the shell base.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Grab the shell lock and bring it to the furnace shell. Insert it firmly into the lock mounting point until it clicks into place. This secures the shell during the charging cycle.","waitForCompletion":true}

actions.Name: VoiceOver
actions.Data: {"text":"Post-tap housekeeping complete. All systems are ready for the next heat cycle. You have successfully completed the EAF steel tapping training.","waitForCompletion":true}

moments.sceneDescription: Industrial EAF steel plant floor with furnace shell at ground level, worker inserting vertical locking mechanism into shell mounting point to secure for next charging cycle

moments.name: Ladle Condition Inspection
moments.userAction: narration
moments.successHint: auto-advance
moments.spatialContext: Player observes Ladle_EAF from tapping pulpit position

moments.name: Verify Ladle Temperature
moments.userAction: Look at PreHeater_Laddle display to confirm 1000-1200C range
moments.successHint: Voice-over confirms temperature is within acceptable range for tapping
moments.spatialContext: PreHeater_Laddle display visible at eye level 2m from player

moments.name: Confirm Ferroalloys Ready
moments.userAction: Inspect Hopper_FeAlloys to verify material loaded
moments.successHint: Voice-over confirms ferroalloys are loaded and ready for addition
moments.spatialContext: Hopper_FeAlloys bunkers visible above ladle position

moments.name: Turn On Local Control Switch
moments.userAction: Rotate LocalControls to ON position
moments.successHint: Switch clicks into ON position, indicator light activates, voice-over confirms control enabled
moments.spatialContext: LocalControls switch on control panel at chest height 1m ahead

moments.name: Move Ladle Transfer Car
moments.userAction: Press EAFTapping button to position LadleCar_EAF under Furnace
moments.successHint: Button press triggers car movement animation, voice-over confirms ladle positioned under furnace
moments.spatialContext: EAFTapping button on control panel, LadleCar_EAF moves into position below Furnace

moments.name: Position Material Addition Chute
moments.userAction: Rotate RCH01CWCCW selector switch to CCW position
moments.successHint: Selector switch rotates to CCW, chute animation moves to tapping position, voice-over confirms ready
moments.spatialContext: RCH01CWCCW selector on control panel at waist height, chute visible overhead

moments.name: Verify Bath Temperature and Composition
moments.userAction: Use SamplingPipe_EAF and check Display_FurnaceTemp for temperature reading
moments.successHint: Display shows 1630°C ±10°C, voice-over confirms bath ready for tapping
moments.spatialContext: Display_FurnaceTemp on monitoring panel at eye level, SamplingPipe_EAF near furnace access

moments.name: Unlock Furnace Frame Locks
moments.userAction: Press Unlock_02 to release furnace frame locks
moments.successHint: Button press triggers unlock sound, indicator shows unlocked state, voice-over confirms frame released
moments.spatialContext: Unlock_02 button on control panel under FRAME label at chest height

moments.name: Enable Tilting Control
moments.userAction: Rotate TiltEnable selector switch to ON position
moments.successHint: Selector switch clicks to ON, tilt-enable indicator illuminates, voice-over confirms tilting enabled
moments.spatialContext: TiltEnable selector switch on control panel at waist height 1m ahead

moments.name: Start Ladle Stirring
moments.userAction: Select NitrogenSelection gas type and activate SterLine1 and SterLine2 switches
moments.successHint: Gas selector clicks to Nitrogen, line switches activate with indicator lights, voice-over confirms stirring started
moments.spatialContext: Gas selector and STIR line switches grouped on control panel at chest height

moments.name: Unlock EBT Flap
moments.userAction: Press ExtrastrokeBYpass and Unlock_01 buttons simultaneously
moments.successHint: Both buttons depress together, unlock sound plays, voice-over confirms EBT flap unlocked
moments.spatialContext: Both buttons on control panel under EBT movement label, spaced 15cm apart at chest height

moments.name: Open EBT Flap
moments.userAction: Press ExtrastrokeBYpass and Opening buttons simultaneously to release steel flow
moments.successHint: Buttons depress together, EBT opens with animation, molten steel flows into ladle, voice-over confirms tapping started
moments.spatialContext: Buttons on control panel near EBT controls, Opening button adjacent to ExtrastrokeBYpass

moments.name: Observe Metal Flow
moments.userAction: narration
moments.successHint: auto-advance
moments.spatialContext: Player observes steel flow from Furnace into Ladle_EAF

moments.name: Emergency Lancing
moments.userAction: Grab LancingPipe and lance open EBT hole if steel does not flow
moments.successHint: Player grabs lance, inserts into EBT hole, steel flow resumes, voice-over confirms emergency procedure completed
moments.spatialContext: LancingPipe available on tool rack at waist height 1.5m from player, EBT access point below furnace

moments.name: Add Ferroalloys at 20-25 Tons
moments.userAction: Press BinShip01SlideGateOpen and BinShip02SlideGateOpen to discharge ferroalloys
moments.successHint: Both buttons press, slide gates open with animation, ferroalloys discharge into ladle, voice-over confirms addition at correct weight
moments.spatialContext: Gate open buttons on control panel at chest height, bunkers visible overhead discharging material

moments.name: Retract Material Addition Chute
moments.userAction: Rotate RCH01CWCCW selector switch to CW position
moments.successHint: Selector rotates to CW, chute animation moves to parking position, voice-over confirms chute clear of tilt path
moments.spatialContext: RCH01CWCCW selector on control panel at waist height, chute retracts overhead

moments.name: Tapping Under Arcing: Lift Electrodes
moments.userAction: Open FurnaceBreakerKey02 then use ElectrodesHydraulicArm to lift Electrodes and lock arms
moments.successHint: Breaker opens with switch action, hydraulic arm controls lift electrodes smoothly, locking sound confirms arms secure, voice-over confirms safe to tilt
moments.spatialContext: FurnaceBreakerKey02 on main panel at chest height, ElectrodesHydraulicArm controls adjacent, Electrodes visible rising above Furnace

moments.name: Tilt Furnace to Complete Tapping
moments.userAction: Operate FurnaceTiltingKey joystick to tilt Furnace toward tapping side
moments.successHint: Joystick movement tilts furnace smoothly, remaining steel flows into ladle, voice-over confirms tapping progressing
moments.spatialContext: FurnaceTiltingKey joystick on control panel at waist height, Furnace tilts visibly toward ladle

moments.name: Tilt Furnace Back (5-7 Tons Remaining)
moments.userAction: Use FurnaceTiltingKey to tilt Furnace back to deslagging side at 150-155 tons
moments.successHint: Joystick tilts furnace back smoothly, Display_FurnaceTemp shows 150-155 tons tapped, voice-over confirms slag carryover avoided
moments.spatialContext: Same joystick controls furnace tilt in reverse direction, weight display visible on monitoring panel

moments.name: Move Ladle Away from Furnace
moments.userAction: Press EAFPreheatChargePosition to move LadleCar_EAF to preheat/charge position
moments.successHint: Button press initiates car movement animation away from furnace, voice-over confirms ladle clear
moments.spatialContext: EAFPreheatChargePosition button on control panel, LadleCar_EAF moves away from tapping zone

moments.name: Return Furnace to 7 Degree Tapping Position
moments.userAction: Use FurnaceTiltingKey to tilt Furnace to 7° tapping position
moments.successHint: Joystick tilts furnace to 7° angle, angle indicator confirms position, voice-over confirms ready for EBT cleaning
moments.spatialContext: FurnaceTiltingKey joystick on control panel, Furnace tilts to precise angle visible on display

moments.name: Clean EBT Bottom
moments.userAction: Cycle ExtrastrokeBYpass with Opening then ExtrastrokeBYpass with Closing to clean EBT
moments.successHint: Button sequence opens then closes EBT flap, cleaning animation plays, voice-over confirms EBT bottom cleaned
moments.spatialContext: All buttons on control panel under EBT movement label at chest height

moments.name: Close and Lock EBT Flap
moments.userAction: Press Closing button then Lock_02 to secure EBT flap
moments.successHint: Closing button closes flap with animation, Lock_02 button secures with locking sound, voice-over confirms EBT sealed
moments.spatialContext: Closing and Lock_02 buttons on control panel under EBT movement label, spaced 10cm apart

moments.name: Open EBT Cover, Inspect, Sand-Fill, Close
moments.userAction: Press Opening for EBT cover, observe Display_FurnaceTemp for inspection, then press Closing for EBT cover
moments.successHint: Opening button opens EBT cover, camera view shows tap hole on display, voice-over narrates sand charging sequence, Closing button closes cover, voice-over confirms EBT sealed and filled
moments.spatialContext: EBT cover controls on panel, Display_FurnaceTemp shows camera feed at eye level, sand charging narrated via VO

moments.name: Check Furnace Condition and Hot Heel
moments.userAction: Observe Display_FurnaceTemp to verify hot heel quantity (~30 tons)
moments.successHint: Display shows remaining steel quantity, voice-over confirms approximately 30 tons hot heel retained for next heat
moments.spatialContext: Display_FurnaceTemp on monitoring panel at eye level showing furnace interior and weight data

moments.name: Return Furnace to Horizontal
moments.userAction: Use FurnaceTiltingKey to tilt Furnace back to horizontal position
moments.successHint: Joystick returns furnace to level, angle indicator confirms 0° horizontal, voice-over confirms furnace reset
moments.spatialContext: FurnaceTiltingKey joystick on control panel, Furnace returns to level position

moments.name: Clean Slag Door and Insert Frame Locks
moments.userAction: narration
moments.successHint: auto-advance
moments.spatialContext: Voice-over narrates slag door cleaning procedure and optional Lock_01 press if scrap charging needed

objectives: Safely tap molten steel from EAF into ladles
Verify ladle preparation and temperature requirements
Execute proper tapping sequence with or without arcing
Control steel flow and tilting to avoid slag carryover
Add ferroalloys and materials at correct timing
Clean and close EBT properly after tapping
Inspect and maintain equipment during tapping operations
Respond to emergency situations during tapping
procedures: Inspect ladle condition and verify temperature range 1000°C to 1200°C with visual color chart
Ensure ferroalloys and slag forming materials are ready in bunkers
Turn on local control switch from EAF tapping pulpit to ON position
Move ladle transfer car to tapping position by pressing EAF tapping position button
Position material addition chute to tapping position by moving selector switch RCH01 to CCW
Verify bath temperature and chemical composition requirements are achieved
Unlock furnace frame locks
Turn on local control switch and enable tilting control by rotating TILT-ENABLE selector switch to ON
Start ladle stirring by selecting gas type (Nitrogen or Argon) and increase rinsing gas pressure using STIR Line 1 and STIR Line 2 switches
Unlock EBT flap by pressing Extra Stroke By-Pass button and EBT Unlock button simultaneously
Open EBT flap by pressing Extra Stroke By-Pass button and EBT Opening button simultaneously
Observe EBT sand dropping and metal flow from furnace into ladle
If steel doesn't flow, use Oxygen lance to open EBT while wearing aluminum jacket
When 20-25 tons tapped, add ferroalloys by pressing BIN SHP01 and SHP02 Slide Gate open buttons
Move material addition chute to parking position using selector switch RCH01 to CW after adding materials
For tapping under arcing: lift electrodes to tapping position and lock arms after opening main circuit breaker
Tilt furnace gradually using joystick to complete tapping
When 5-7 tons remain to desired weight (aim 150-155 tons), tilt furnace back to deslagging side
Move ladle from below furnace by pressing ladle car EAF PREHEAT CHARG.POSITION button
Tilt furnace to tapping position at 7° angle
Clean EBT bottom by closing and opening flap using Extra Stroke By-Pass and Opening-Closing buttons
Close EBT flap and lock by pressing LOCK button under EBT movement label
Open EBT cover by pressing EBT COVER OPEN button
Inspect tap hole using camera through monitoring display
Fill EBT with sand by pressing EBT SAND CHARGING OPEN button until sand forms dome shape
Close sand hopper by pressing EBT SAND CHARGING CLOSE button
Close EBT cover by pressing EBT COVER CLOSE button
Check furnace condition and estimate hot heel quantity (average 30 tons)
Tilt furnace to horizontal position using joystick
Clean slag door ensuring fettling material height is 10-15 cm above slag apron
Insert shell verticality locks by pressing LOCK button under FRAME label if scrap or electrode change needed
sopContext.constraints: Use only tools and equipment in good condition and appropriate for the task. Check tools to ensure protective devices, machine guards and alarms are functional. Follow all area safety warning signs. Maintain proper housekeeping. Report hazards, near-misses, or incidents immediately to Supervisor. Any member can stop activity if unsafe conditions found and must inform shift Supervisor immediately. All employees must comply with safety regulations and use proper PPE. Do not charge any material in ladle before tapping. Supervisor and Senior Melter must inspect ladle cleaning and temperature before tapping. Stay at least 10 feet radius from suspended loads. Pay attention to crane siren and load travel path. Ensure floor is clean and free from obstructions. Good cable management required - organize trailing cords/cables/tools/materials, keep floors dry and free from water, grease, slippery liquids. All hand tools must be stored in toolbox. EBT must be changed or repaired if tapping time is equal or less than 2.5 min. Ladle temperature must be 1000°C to 1200°C range. Ladle top ring must be cleaned before sending to EAF and must be in level condition after filling nozzle sand. Ladle should not exceed more than 3 minutes under EAF before start tapping to avoid excessive temperature drop. Steel weight must be reset to zero before tapping. Material addition chute must be in tapping position before tapping to avoid discharging ferroalloys on ground. Furnace frame locks must be unlocked. DRI movable chute must be working properly. Only trained and authorized persons must operate. Labels must be clearly readable. Defective parts must be replaced. Tapping under arcing can only be performed during charging hot DRI. Projected EBT tapping time must be above 3 min for tapping under arcing. Furnace must be kept in horizontal position during tapping under arcing. Tapping under arcing should not exceed 25% of molten steel inside ladle (40-ton maximum). Ladle addition chute should be moved to parking position before reaching 2° tilting angle. Required tapping temperature for ladles in circulation is 1630°C +/- 10°C. For ladles out of circulation and new ladles is 1650°C +/- 10°C. Addition of Alloys and Recarburizer on bottom of ladle is not allowed as it will boil. Do not add Recarburizer before adding ferroalloys material as it will lead to boiling inside ladle. Tilting of furnace should be done by joystick to control tilting and avoid liquid metal contact with water cooled panels above tap hole which can cause panel damage and water leak/explosion. When approximately 5 to 7 tons remaining to desired tapping weight (aim 150-155 tons) furnace must be tilted back to deslagging side to avoid slag carryover into ladle. Minimum free board for ladle is around 400-500 mm. Oxygen lance must have back fire arrestor and holder must always be pressed if pipe is inserted inside tap hole until taken out to prevent pipe blockage by metal leading to back fire and burn injury. Lancing pipe must be replaced after each use. Lancing EBT from upside is not allowed. Average Hot Heel should be around 30 ton. Fettling material on slag door should not be higher than 10-15 cm above slag apron. Not more than 20kg to be carried by hand. Maintain proper posture - lift with legs not with back. Face toward direction of shoveled load throw to avoid twisting. Do not pick up more than 10 pounds with any shovel at one time. In emergency situations with power failure or requiring tilting more than 10° angle, emergency stick can be used but extra care must be taken to control smoothly and avoid jerking which can lead to electrode breakages.
